Text:
One single continuous photograph — no panels, grids, or split frames.

Use the uploaded photo as the exact base composition. The street in the source
is empty — populate it with the people and vehicles specified below. Remove all
modern businesses, logos, brands, storefront names, banners, advertisements, and
signage visible in the source image.

PRESERVE (must match source exactly)
- camera: eye-level, facing intersection, fov 90
- residential road, 1-lane, concrete
- sidewalks present, curbs present
- driveways: right, center; parking: lot
- commercial building at left, 1 story, stucco, glass, brick, flat roof, shallow setback
- utilities: street lamp
- landscape: grass strip, hedge, flower bed, mulch
Keep this location instantly recognizable.

OUTPUT FORMAT
Create a TRUE 9:16 vertical portrait image — one single frame. Expand the SAME
scene naturally above (sky) and below (street/pavement) — this is canvas
extension of one photograph, not additional panels. Do not stretch or distort
the source geometry.

TRANSFORM TO 1985
Reagan era prosperity. A 1985 self-service gas station — digital price sign newly installed, a small convenience store where the second service bay used to be.

PERIOD DETAILS
- self-service standard, full service rare
- ice freezer chest with an 'ICE $1' decal by the store door
- price sign showing gas around $1.12
- main sign: an independent station sign in the style of Shell
Typography: bold geometric sans-serif, early computer-influenced fonts.
Sign text is only what appears in quotes — do not turn other words from this prompt into signage.- window signs: 'MOTOR OIL 99¢ QT', 'CIGARETTES $1.10 PACK'
- an 'AIR & WATER' sign at the lot corner
- a mailbox with graffiti scratches at the lot edge
Typography: bold geometric sans-serif, early computer-influenced fonts.

PEOPLE
EXACTLY 4 people, spread out with empty areas.
- a driver waiting in his car for a free pump
- a customer walking into the store counting bills
- a group of teenagers in bright windbreakers
Clothing: men in acid wash jeans, polo shirts with collars up; women in bright neon colors, leg warmers. Fashion palette: neon pink, electric blue, purple. No posing or eye contact. All people on sidewalks, at storefronts, or beside parked vehicles — the driving lanes stay empty of pedestrians.

VEHICLES
EXACTLY 4 period vehicles, all different:
- 1982-1988 Chevrolet Celebrity — boxy front-wheel drive sedan — charcoal gray
- 1975-1991 Ford Econoline — boxy full-size van — silver
- 1980-1985 Buick LeSabre — boxy full-size, chrome trim — beige
- 1979-1985 Ford Mustang — Fox body, angular hatchback coupe — burgundy
Parked with gaps; no vehicle newer than 1985.
PLACEMENT: the first two parked on the RIGHT side; one parked on the left near the corner; the last one driving toward the camera in the far lane. No vehicle in the same spot as any other era.

ENVIRONMENT
- road markings: yellow center line, white edge lines, painted crosswalks
- utilities: overhead power lines standard, wooden utility poles

TREES
- deciduous tree at left: very young sapling
- deciduous tree at background: young tree, thin trunk
- deciduous tree at right: young tree, thin trunk
Tree sizes MUST follow this specification even where they differ from the source photo.

PHOTOGRAPHIC STYLE
COLOR photograph — Kodacolor VR 200 or Fujicolor 100 look.
Color: slightly cool tones compared to 1970s. Grain: moderate grain, cleaner than 1975.
Photorealistic — NOT black-and-white, no HDR.

PRIVACY
Blur all faces and license plates.
SelectedVehicles: 1982-1988 Chevrolet Celebrity — boxy front-wheel drive sedan
1980-1985 Buick LeSabre — boxy full-size, chrome trim
1979-1985 Ford Mustang — Fox body, angular hatchback coupe
